name: United Arab Emirates
summary: Day 63 Thursday. UAE OPEC EXIT TAKES EFFECT TOMORROW MAY 1 (CALENDAR INFLECTION ~24 HOURS AWAY); JEDDAH GCC SUMMIT SAUDI-UAE RIFT VISIBLE — UAE SENT FM NOT PRESIDENT (DIPLOMATIC SNUB); SAUDI INFORMATION POSTURE DEFENSIVE 3 DAYS RUNNING. (1) GCC consultative summit took place Apr 28 in Jeddah chaired by Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman (Al Jazeera, The National UAE, Soufan Center, US News, Organiser). DIPLOMATIC-SIGNIFICANCE DATAPOINT: UAE sent its Foreign Minister, NOT its President, to a summit at the host's most important bilateral relationship — a VISIBLE SNUB. UAE Energy Minister Al Mazrouei separately confirmed UAE did NOT consult Saudi Arabia or any other OPEC country before the May 1 exit announcement. (2) Saudi former oil adviser Mohammad al-Sabban publicly downplayed via Al Jazeera ('not a major blow, one country going out doesn't mean anything') — but US News analysis 'UAE Oil Break Exposes Deepening Saudi Rift as Gulf Power Shifts' frames Riyadh as having 'no good public options.' MBS chaired Jeddah but did NOT produce consensus communique on either the war or the OPEC fracture. The 'Saudi formal response to UAE OPEC exit within 14 days' watching trigger from yesterday's brief is at Day 3 of 14 with no MBS direct statement. Saudi conspicuous-quiet has now persisted Tue-Wed-Thu (3 days), crossing threshold from 'considering response' to 'no good response.' (3) Soufan Center IntelBrief calls it 'Gulf Shakeup.' Atlantic Council framing of 'Petrodollar Crisis' and 'end of Gulf solidarity' (Al Jazeera op-ed). [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]

active_triggers.condition: UAE enters war as combatant (not just defensive)
UAE adds 500K-1M bpd of supply within 6 months of OPEC exit (May 1) confirming Atlantic Council/Derentz medium-term-bearish thesis
Saudi Arabia issues formal public response to UAE OPEC exit within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Iran struck US Consulate in Dubai, Dubai International Airport, and Fairmont The Palm hotel in initial retaliation wave

5 signals. ADNOC Ruwais refinery (UAE's largest, 922K bpd capacity) shut down after Iranian drone strike caused fire | Iran struck Shah gas field (set ablaze) and Fujairah port (key oil hub used as Hormuz bypass) | IRGC threatened US university campuses in Gulf on March 29, specifically naming NYU Abu Dhabi

15 signals. UAE described as 'perhaps the most hawkish' among Gulf allies, pushing for ground invasion of Iran (WaPo national security desk) | Al-Salmi VLCC (Kuwait-flagged, 2M bbl Saudi/Kuwaiti oil for China) struck at Dubai port anchorage March 31 — most significant single-vessel | CONFIRMED: Borouge petrochemical plant ($60B, world's largest single-site polyolefin plant) SHUT DOWN operations at Ruwais Industrial Comple
